reasoning: The timeframe is very short: only about 38 days between the knowledge cutoff (April 23, 2025) and the resolution date (June 1, 2025). The CDC just updated its Travel Health Notice for Yellow Fever in Colombia on April 15, 2025, maintaining it at Level 2. Because Yellow Fever has an extremely effective and widely available vaccine, the CDC typically uses a Level 2 notice ("Practice Enhanced Precautions") to ensure travelers get vaccinated. A Level 3 notice ("Reconsider Travel/Avoid Non-essential Travel") is generally reserved for situations where traveler risk cannot be mitigated (e.g., no vaccine available) or where healthcare infrastructure completely collapses. While Colombia declared a national health emergency on April 17, 2025, this is a local measure to enforce mass vaccination efforts and travel controls rather than an indicator of unmitigable risk to vaccinated US travelers. Although there is one suspected breakthrough case under investigation that could hint at vaccine evasion, resolving this and confirming a mutant strain capable of widespread breakthrough within the next 5 weeks is highly improbable. Further, the question title says "above level 3" while the text says "greater than 2" (Level 3+); regardless of which applies, the CDC is very unlikely to raise the notice to Level 3 or 4 in this short window.
